steps.title: Identify the text focus
steps.content: The text introduces Lind and colleagues’ study about ocean acidification and fish behavior, then immediately raises a concern: the study relied on a mean sample size of only about 14 fish. Soderberg and colleagues warn that small samples can increase biased analysis and exaggerated effects.

steps.title: Eliminate unsupported purposes
steps.content: Choice A invents a debate about the cause of ocean acidification. Choice B claims the text discusses an overlooked aspect of ocean acidification, but the focus is study reliability. Choice D says the text explains changes in fish behavior over time, which it does not. The purpose is to note a concern about a study’s findings. The answer is C.
